name: Tootsie Roll Industries
notes: Family-controlled, dual-class, mature U.S. confectionery niche; long buyback record. Closest non-LIFO analog to Seneca's governance and capital-allocation side.

name: John B. Sanfilippo & Son
notes: Owner-operator with founding-family dual-class; tree-nut private-label / branded mix similar in tone to Seneca's vegetable-canning posture; special dividends more than buybacks.

name: Bridgford Foods
notes: Family-controlled micro-cap; structurally similar in float / coverage profile, narrower category.

name: Lifeway Foods
notes: Family-controlled small-cap food name; less LIFO-exposed than Seneca, more growth-oriented.

name: Hooker Furnishings
notes: Direct LIFO-mechanism analog; family control absent.

name: Olympic Steel
notes: Strong LIFO-reserve accounting analog; cyclicality higher than Seneca; Siegal family historically large holder.

name: Friedman Industries
notes: Micro-cap LIFO steel processor; useful for LIFO + undercovered combination.

name: Insteel Industries
notes: Steady-niche steel-wire supplier; useful for the durable-niche + buyback feature combination.

name: Ethan Allen Interiors
notes: LIFO furniture with vertically integrated assets; coverage thin.

name: Greif, Inc.
notes: Dual-class industrial packaging with concentrated voting in B class; thicker coverage than Seneca but mechanism-fit for control / asset / buyback features.

name: Crawford & Company
notes: Dual-class controlled small-cap; mechanism overlap on undercovered + family control + buyback.
